(born December 17, 1983) is a retired [American](/wiki/United_States "United States") [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts"). A professional competitor from 2006 until 2013, he competed for the [UFC](/wiki/UFC "UFC") and [Strikeforce](/wiki/Strikeforce_(mixed_martial_arts) "Strikeforce (mixed martial arts)").

**John Cholish** (born December 17, 1983) is a retired [American](/wiki/United_States "United States") [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts"). A professional competitor from 2006 until 2013, he competed for the [UFC](/wiki/UFC "UFC") and [Strikeforce](/wiki/Strikeforce_(mixed_martial_arts) "Strikeforce (mixed martial arts)").

## Mixed martial arts career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Cholish&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts career")]

### Early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Cholish&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Early career")]

Cholish made his professional MMA debut in September 2007.[[1]](#cite_note-1) He lost his first fight via submission, but won his next seven fights before signing with the UFC.

### Strikeforce

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Cholish&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Strikeforce")]

Cholish made his [Strikeforce](/wiki/Strikeforce_(mixed_martial_arts) "Strikeforce (mixed martial arts)") debut in February 2011 as part of the preliminary card for [Strikeforce: Fedor vs. Silva](/wiki/Strikeforce:_Fedor_vs._Silva "Strikeforce: Fedor vs. Silva"). He defeated [The Ultimate Fighter](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter "The Ultimate Fighter") veteran [Marc Stevens](/wiki/Marc_Stevens_(fighter) "Marc Stevens (fighter)") via submission (kneebar) in the second round. Cholish drew considerable attention from MMA news sites for the submission due to its rarity.

### Ultimate Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Cholish&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Ultimate Fighting Championship")]

Cholish made his promotional debut against [Mitch Clarke](/wiki/Mitch_Clarke "Mitch Clarke") on December 10, 2011, at [UFC 140](/wiki/UFC_140 "UFC 140").[[2]](#cite_note-2) He won the fight via TKO in the second round.

Cholish faced [Danny Castillo](/wiki/Danny_Castillo "Danny Castillo") on May 5, 2012, at [UFC on Fox 3](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_3 "UFC on Fox 3").[[3]](#cite_note-3) He was defeated by Castillo via unanimous decision.

Cholish was expected to face [Yves Edwards](/wiki/Yves_Edwards "Yves Edwards") on December 8, 2012, at [UFC on Fox 5](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_5 "UFC on Fox 5").[[4]](#cite_note-4) However, Cholish was forced out of the bout with a groin injury and replaced by [Jeremy Stephens](/wiki/Jeremy_Stephens "Jeremy Stephens").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Cholish next faced [Gleison Tibau](/wiki/Gleison_Tibau "Gleison Tibau") on May 18, 2013, at [UFC on FX 8](/wiki/UFC_on_FX_8 "UFC on FX 8").[[6]](#cite_note-6) He lost the fight via submission in the second round. After the fight Cholish retired from MMA, citing low pay as the reason for his retirement.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

> "I'm fortunate enough that I have a job that provides for me really well, I give a lot of these guys credit that fight at this level. I think they could be compensated much better based on the income that the UFC takes in. Fortunately, I can just walk away and I'm OK with it. By no means do I mean it disrespectfully toward any other fighters because I think they do a great job. But hopefully Zuffa and the UFC will start paying them a little better."

— John Cholish, Interview after retirement

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Cholish&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Cholish, a Cornell University graduate, holds a full-time job with the Marex Spectron which brokers commodities of natural gas crude oil on the institutional platform.[[8]](#cite_note-8)
